one_liner: Airtable offers a well-documented REST Web API for managing bases, tables, and records, authenticated via personal access tokens or OAuth2, self-serve on the Free plan with rate/usage limits.

auth_methods.notes: Personal access tokens (PAT) passed as Authorization: Bearer header; self-serve creation at /create/tokens, scoped to specific bases/workspaces.
OAuth access tokens for third-party integrations where end-users grant access; registered at /create/oauth, standard OAuth2 grant flow.

self_serve_notes: Free plan includes API access with a limit of 1,000 API calls/month per workspace; rate limited to 5 req/sec (50 req/sec with PATs). Tokens can be created immediately via developer portal without approval.

api_surface.notes: Covers base/table/record CRUD, metadata API (create/list tables, bases), scopes system, webhooks, and per-base dynamic API docs.

main_blocker: None significant; Free plan API call limits (1,000/month) may require paid plan for higher-volume agent usage.

evidence.supports: Token-based auth via Bearer header, PAT and OAuth token types described
Self-serve PAT creation at /create/tokens with configurable scopes and resource access
OAuth authorization endpoint and flow for third-party integrations
Free plan includes API access with monthly call limits, confirming self-serve free tier

flags: Multiple community-built MCP servers exist (npm packages) rather than an official Airtable-maintained MCP server
Free plan API call limit (1,000/month) is quite low for production agent workloads
